name: Zapier
what_it_does: No-code automation platform connecting 5000+ apps via Zaps (trigger → action workflows), with an API for triggering Zap steps and Zapier NLA for natural language-driven automation.
use_cases: Triggering Zapier webhook-in steps to fire automated workflows from agent actions
Using Zapier NLA (Natural Language Actions) to let AI agents control user-authorized app actions
Automating CRM/email/calendar actions via Zapier when direct API integration is not available
Agent-triggered data routing: new DB row → Zap → email/Slack/spreadsheet
Low-code integration for long-tail SaaS apps that lack direct APIs
not_for: High-volume automated workflows — Zapier is not designed for >1000 tasks/minute at reasonable cost
Complex branching workflows requiring code (use n8n or Make instead)
Real-time sub-second integrations (Zapier has minutes-level latency)
best_when: You need to quickly integrate with one of 5000+ apps without building a custom API integration, especially for business users' personal app authorizations via NLA.
avoid_when: You need high throughput, complex logic, or sub-minute latency — use direct API integration or n8n/Make for workflow automation at scale.

auth.notes: API key for Zapier NLA and developer API. OAuth2 for Zapier platform integrations. NLA uses user OAuth to authorize actions on user's behalf.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter: $19.99/month for 750 tasks; Professional: $49/month for 2000 tasks; Team: $69/month
pricing.notes: Each automated action = 1 task. Multi-step Zaps use multiple tasks. Costs scale rapidly with usage.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; webhook-triggered Zaps are faster than polling

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Zap execution logs in dashboard; webhook responses don't include Zap execution status
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each webhook call triggers a new Zap run; no deduplication
known_agent_gotchas: Webhook-triggered Zaps return 200 immediately without waiting for Zap execution — fire-and-forget with no execution confirmation
Zapier NLA requires user OAuth — agents cannot use NLA on behalf of users who haven't connected their accounts
Free tier polling is 15-minute intervals — near-real-time requires paid plan with webhook triggers
Task count limits are hard stops — when limit exceeded, Zaps silently fail; monitor task usage for production agents
Zap steps run in sequence with no parallelism — multi-step workflows with external API calls can take 30+ seconds

security_score_components.security_notes: User OAuth delegated access is narrow-scoped per app. Webhook secrets for Zap trigger verification. Data passes through Zapier servers.
